content.problemStatement:
Given nnn (nnn is **even**), determine the number of **black cells** in an n×nn \times nn×n chessboard. 

Update the method given in the IDE to solve the problem.


Input Format
The only line of the input contains a single integer nnn.



Output Format
Output the number of black cells in an n×nn \times nn×n chessboard.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single even integer n (2 ≤ n ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of black cells on an n×n chessboard.

sampleTestCases.explanation: An 8×8 board has 64 cells. Half of them are black, so 64/2 = 32.
A 2×2 board has 4 cells, half black → 4/2 = 2.

solution.approach: A chessboard of size n×n has n² cells. Because n is even, the board can be perfectly divided into black and white cells, each occupying exactly half of the cells. Therefore the number of black cells is (n * n) / 2. The algorithm reads each n, computes n*n using 64‑bit integer arithmetic to avoid overflow, divides by 2, and prints the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
